title: Rhynchospora rufa
taxonomy: Rhynchospora rufa is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. The genus Rhynchospora is part of the larger family Cyperaceae, which includes a diverse group of monocots. Rhynchospora rufa is classified as a member of the order Poales, which encompasses various families of grass-like plants. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its grass-like leaves and stems. Further classification places Rhynchospora rufa within the family Cyperaceae, which is a distinct group of monocotyledonous plants.

intro: Rhynchospora rufa, commonly known as red beak sedge, is a type of perennial sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rhynchospora rufa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
